name: r2pipe (Radare2 Python Scripting API)

performance.notes: IPC pipe to radare2 process; analysis speed depends on binary complexity

agent_readiness.notes: Python scripting for radare2 reverse engineering framework. import r2pipe. r2 = r2pipe.open('binary'). r2.cmd('aaa') # analyze all. r2.cmdj('aflj') # function list as JSON. r2.cmdj('pdj 20') # disassemble 20 instructions as JSON. r2.cmd('s main; pdf') # seek to main and print disassembly. JSON output variants (cmdj) for programmatic access. Remote analysis: r2pipe.open('r2://host:port'). Automation of binary analysis, malware research, CTF solving. radare2 must be installed.

gotchas: Command interface is radare2 command language — steep learning curve
JSON output from cmdj can be unpredictable for complex structures
radare2 itself has high memory usage for large binaries
best_when: Automated binary analysis pipelines in Python
CTF binary exploitation with programmatic disassembly
Malware research automation (batch analysis of samples)
Scripting radare2 analysis without writing radare2 scripts directly
avoid_when: Reverse engineering requiring good UI (Ghidra or Binary Ninja better)
When pwntools covers the binary interaction needs
summary: r2pipe provides Python scripting for the radare2 reverse engineering framework. r2pipe.open('binary').cmd('aaa').cmdj('aflj') — runs radare2 commands and parses JSON output. Automates binary analysis, disassembly, function listing. radare2 must be installed separately. Steep learning curve requires radare2 command knowledge. Used in CTF automation and malware batch analysis.
